summaryForNext: All 4 goals MET (G1 shared cross-replica OAuth rate-limit, G2 delegate observability + no-re-stamp, G3 exposure guardrails, G4 E2E vs real Ory Hydra). QA 4/4 PASS first-pass, zero blocking-constraint violations. Separated security review caught 1 HIGH (rate-limit header-rotation bypass -> keyed on client_id) + 1 MEDIUM (introspection-delegate redirect-SSRF -> no-redirect parity) + LOWs, all fixed pre-archive. 378->406 core tests. Sycophancy gate: PASS (s08=false, score 0.0). Open debt: (1) Redis-outage posture is startup-only (token endpoint degrades to per-replica governor at runtime); (2) local-mint exchange path unmetered; (3) Hydra-outage deny paths unit-tested not E2E; (4) OAuth anon-bucket sharing. Recommended next phase: agent-gateway-mcp-tool-governance -- per-tool-call authz + Cedar ergonomics FIRST (the core agent-gateway differentiator), then runtime agent budget on the shared Redis counters (closes delegate-budget-bypass debt), MCP tool-call observability, optionally strict cross-replica rate-limit mode. Stay authorization-first; federate any JWKS IdM, never an IdP; LLM-ops bundle out of scope.
